Title: In the grove of cedars, Lebanon. The group of cedars occupies the top of a hill with five culminating points of various sizes. The rock on which the trees grow is white limestone, and the decaying spines, cones, and other matter have formed a dark-coloured soil. In the background rise the snow-covered peaks of Dahr el Kodîb; the highest point is ten thousand and fifty feet above the sea-level.
Artist: Wilson, Charles William, Sir (1836-1905); Fenn, Harry (1838-1911); Johnstone, J.
